design_summary.name: RV32EC_Zmmul CPU
design_summary.description: Machine-mode RV32E+C+Zmmul core with fetch, decode, execute/writeback, CSR/trap, load/store, multiplier, and optional SPI boot assist integrated through an observable top-level wrapper.

modules.name: alu32_branch_unit
modules.description: Combinational ALU, compare, branch condition, and branch/jump target generator used by the execute stage.

behavior_contract.reset_behavior: No reset state. Outputs are pure combinational functions of current inputs.
behavior_contract.handshake_protocol: No handshake. Inputs may change at any time and outputs must settle without requiring a clock edge.
behavior_contract.output_timing: result_o, branch_taken_o, and branch_target_o respond in the same cycle after combinational settle.
behavior_contract.retention_rules: No internal retention. Outputs must not depend on prior input history.
behavior_contract.completion_semantics: Every input combination produces a fully defined ALU result and branch decision/target.
forbidden_assumptions: Do not assume op_b_i is pre-shift-masked beyond standard 5-bit shift use.
Do not assume branch_op_i is zero when alu_op_i is active; both outputs must remain defined.
Do not assume signed and unsigned compares are interchangeable.

modules.name: multiplier_unit
modules.description: Sequential Zmmul multiply engine with start/busy/done handshake and low-32-bit product result.

behavior_contract.reset_behavior: Active-low asynchronous reset clears busy_o, done_o, and result_o to zero and abandons any in-flight multiply.
state_elements: latched operands or product pipeline state
behavior_contract.handshake_protocol: A multiply starts when start_i is sampled high while the unit is idle. busy_o remains asserted during the operation. done_o pulses for one completion cycle when the low-32-bit product is available on result_o.
behavior_contract.output_timing: busy_o changes only on clock/reset events. done_o is a one-cycle pulse. result_o becomes valid with done_o and then remains held until the next completed multiply or reset.
behavior_contract.retention_rules: While busy_o is high, the operation context is retained internally and new start_i assertions are ignored until completion.
behavior_contract.completion_semantics: The completed operation returns the low 32 bits of op_a_i * op_b_i using two's-complement bit behavior, regardless of signed interpretation of the source operands.
forbidden_assumptions: Do not assume single-cycle multiply latency.
Do not assume start_i can be accepted while busy_o is high.
Do not assume done_o remains high after the completion pulse.

modules.name: regfile_16x32
modules.description: RV32E integer register file with two read ports, one write port, and hardwired x0.

behavior_contract.reset_behavior: Active-low reset may clear architecturally stored registers to zero if implemented; x0 must read as zero regardless of reset state.
state_elements: 16 x 32-bit architectural register array with x0 hardwired to zero
behavior_contract.handshake_protocol: No handshake. Read ports are continuously address-driven. Writes occur on the active clock edge when we_i is high and waddr_i is nonzero.
behavior_contract.output_timing: rdata1_o and rdata2_o are combinational reads of current architectural state. New written data is observable after the write edge.
behavior_contract.retention_rules: Registers x1-x15 retain values until overwritten or reset. Writes to x0 are ignored.
behavior_contract.completion_semantics: A valid write updates exactly one architectural register per enabled write cycle.
forbidden_assumptions: Do not assume writes to x0 take effect.
Do not assume unspecified power-up values for x1-x15 unless reset was applied.
Do not assume dual-write capability.

modules.name: decode_control_unit
modules.description: Instruction decoder for RV32E, C, CSR/system, load/store, branch/jump, and Zmmul with illegal-instruction detection.

behavior_contract.reset_behavior: No reset state. Decode outputs are pure combinational functions of instr_i and pc_i.
behavior_contract.handshake_protocol: No handshake. An instruction word presented at instr_i is decoded immediately after settle.
behavior_contract.output_timing: All decode fields respond in the same cycle after combinational settle.
behavior_contract.retention_rules: No retained history. Outputs must not depend on previous instructions.
behavior_contract.completion_semantics: For any legal RV32E/C/Zmmul instruction in scope, outputs identify source/destination registers, immediate, class controls, and writeback intent. Unsupported divide/remainder and any x16-x31 references must assert illegal_o.
forbidden_assumptions: Do not assume all inputs are 32-bit base ISA instructions; compressed forms must be recognized from instr_i[1:0].
Do not assume register encodings above x15 are legal.
Do not assume CSR/system instructions always write back.
Do not assume illegal instructions still produce useful execution enables.

modules.name: fetch_unit
modules.description: PC control and instruction buffering block with redirect priority for trap, MRET, branch/jump, and sequential flow.

behavior_contract.reset_behavior: Active-low asynchronous reset clears buffered instruction valid state and loads the current fetch PC from reset_vector_i.
behavior_contract.handshake_protocol: instr_req_o is the level request for the current fetch address. When instr_ready_i is observed, the corresponding instr_rdata_i is captured into the instruction buffer unless a flush/redirect overrides it. Redirect priority is trap, then MRET, then branch/jump, then sequential progression.
behavior_contract.output_timing: instr_addr_o and pc_o reflect the held or redirected fetch PC. instr_valid_o asserts once a fetched instruction has been captured and remains asserted until consumed or flushed.
behavior_contract.retention_rules: When stall_i is high, the PC and buffered instruction state are held. flush_i clears buffered validity. During redirects, stale buffered instructions are invalidated.
behavior_contract.completion_semantics: Sequential PC increments by 2 when is_compressed_i is high for the retiring instruction, otherwise by 4.
forbidden_assumptions: Do not assume zero-wait instruction memory.
Do not assume branch redirect has priority over trap or MRET.
Do not assume buffered data remains valid across flush_i.
Do not require proof of hidden regfile contents, hidden ALU result buses, or hidden CSR state at top level unless exposed by a port.
Treat datapath-class top-level tests as observable integration flows unless the final value is visible at the top interface.

modules.name: csr_trap_controller
modules.description: Machine-mode CSR bank and trap/interrupt redirect controller supporting direct traps and MRET return.

behavior_contract.reset_behavior: Active-low asynchronous reset initializes implemented machine CSRs to defined reset values, clears redirect pulses, and clears stored trap context except any architecturally defined reset default such as mtvec if hardwired.
state_elements: CSR read mux state if registered
behavior_contract.handshake_protocol: CSR accesses occur when csr_en_i is high; writes additionally require csr_we_i. trap_req_i requests synchronous exception entry. ext_irq_i requests asynchronous machine external interrupt entry when not masked. mret_i requests return from trap. Redirect outputs are observable control pulses or one-cycle valids associated with the selected target PC.
behavior_contract.output_timing: csr_rdata_o reflects the addressed CSR after settle or after the relevant clock edge if reads are registered. Redirect valids are asserted in the cycle of accepted trap or MRET handling.
behavior_contract.retention_rules: Trap context CSRs retain their values until overwritten by a later trap, MRET side effects, CSR write, or reset.
behavior_contract.completion_semantics: Trap entry captures trap_pc_i into mepc and cause into mcause, then redirects to mtvec. MRET redirects to mepc. Direct mode only is supported.
forbidden_assumptions: Do not assume vectored mtvec support.
Do not assume nested interrupt preemption.
Do not assume undefined CSR addresses return architecturally meaningful values.
Do not assume ext_irq_i is level-sensitive beyond implementation-defined single entry acceptance.

modules.name: load_store_unit
modules.description: Address generation and variable-latency data memory interface with byte-enable formatting and aligned load return path.

behavior_contract.reset_behavior: Active-low asynchronous reset clears outstanding transaction state, done_o, stall_o, load_data_o, and misaligned_o.
state_elements: latched effective address and access attributes
aligned load return register if registered
behavior_contract.handshake_protocol: A transaction begins when req_i is high. mem_req_o remains asserted while the request is outstanding unless blocked by misalignment trap policy. Completion occurs when mem_ready_i is observed for a bus-issued request, or immediately when a misaligned trap is flagged with trapping enabled.
behavior_contract.output_timing: Address, write-enable, byte enables, and store data reflect the current request. done_o asserts on completion. stall_o is high while a request is outstanding and not yet complete. load_data_o is valid on completed load operations.
behavior_contract.retention_rules: During memory wait states, the request context is retained and outputs hold stable until mem_ready_i or reset. misaligned_o is asserted for the active misaligned request outcome.
behavior_contract.completion_semantics: Load extraction and sign/zero extension are determined by size_i, unsigned_i, and address low bits. If trap_on_misaligned_i is high and alignment is violated, no bus request is issued and completion is immediate with misaligned_o asserted.
forbidden_assumptions: Do not assume single-cycle memory response.
Do not assume word alignment for all accesses.
Do not assume stores produce load_data_o.
Do not assume misaligned accesses are always trapped.

modules.name: boot_rom_loader
modules.description: SPI boot assist FSM that validates header, copies image words into IRAM, returns boot PC, and enters fail-stop on terminal boot error.

behavior_contract.reset_behavior: Active-low asynchronous reset returns the boot FSM to idle, clears done_o and fail_stop_o, clears boot_pc_o, and drops outstanding SPI/IRAM controls.
behavior_contract.handshake_protocol: start_i launches boot when idle. spi_req_o requests a SPI read for spi_addr_o. spi_ready_i qualifies returned spi_rdata_i. IRAM writes are issued with iram_we_o and are accepted when iram_ready_i is high. done_o indicates successful boot completion. fail_stop_o is terminal once asserted.
behavior_contract.output_timing: Control outputs change on clock edges according to FSM state. done_o may pulse or hold in the completed state; fail_stop_o holds once entered.
behavior_contract.retention_rules: Boot progress state is retained across cycles until success, fail-stop, or reset. After fail_stop_o asserts, the module remains in terminal failure state until reset.
behavior_contract.completion_semantics: Successful completion produces boot_pc_o from the image metadata or terminal word as defined by the boot protocol. Invalid header, range violation, validation failure, or timeout/error cause fail-stop and suppress done_o.
forbidden_assumptions: Do not assume all SPI words are immediately available.
Do not assume IRAM always accepts writes without backpressure.
Do not assume validation is disabled.
Do not assume fail-stop can self-clear without reset.

modules.name: execute_writeback_unit
modules.description: Execute-stage controller that arbitrates ALU, multiply, load/store completion, CSR path, branch redirects, traps, and final writeback.

behavior_contract.reset_behavior: Active-low asynchronous reset clears stall_o, branch_taken_o, wb_en_o, trap_req_o, mret_req_o, and any held execution context.
state_elements: held instruction context for multi-cycle memory or multiply operations
behavior_contract.handshake_protocol: Each presented instruction class is interpreted from the current decoded controls. ALU and simple branch/jump operations can complete immediately. Memory operations hold context until load_ready_i indicates completion. Multiply operations hold context until mul_done_i indicates completion. CSR read/writeback uses csr_rdata_i. Illegal instructions generate trap_req_o instead of normal completion.
behavior_contract.output_timing: For single-cycle operations, outputs are valid in the same execution cycle after settle or by the next sampling edge, depending on the enclosing pipeline. stall_o remains asserted while waiting on load_ready_i or mul_done_i. Writeback becomes valid upon operation completion.
behavior_contract.retention_rules: When stalled on memory or multiply, destination register and operation context are retained until completion or reset.
behavior_contract.completion_semantics: branch_taken_o/branch_target_o indicate redirect for taken branches and jumps. wb_en_o, wb_rd_idx_o, and wb_data_o define the single architectural commit point. trap_req_o has priority over writeback. mret_req_o requests trap return without writeback.
forbidden_assumptions: Do not assume load_data_i is valid unless load_ready_i is high.
Do not assume mul_result_i is valid unless mul_done_i is high.
Do not assume illegal_i can coexist with normal writeback.
Do not assume stores write back a destination register.
Do not require proof of hidden regfile contents, hidden ALU result buses, or hidden CSR state at top level unless exposed by a port.
Treat datapath-class top-level tests as observable integration flows unless the final value is visible at the top interface.

modules.name: rv32ec_zmmul_cpu_top
modules.description: Observable top-level integration module that instantiates all architectural sub-blocks and sequences reset, optional SPI boot, fetch, execute, traps, interrupts, memory stalls, and fail-stop behavior.

behavior_contract.reset_behavior: Active-low asynchronous reset clears fail-stop state, clears in-flight CPU/boot operations, selects reset vector behavior, and begins either normal fetch or SPI boot sequencing after reset release depending on boot_mode_spi.
state_elements: front-end pipeline state via instantiated fetch
outstanding memory and multiply wait state context
behavior_contract.handshake_protocol: Top-level instruction fetch, data memory, and SPI boot interfaces are the only observable protocol surfaces. instr_req/instr_addr drive instruction fetch. data_req/data_we/data_be/data_addr/data_wdata drive variable-latency data access and remain stable across waits. spi_req/spi_addr drive boot reads until boot completion or fail-stop. ext_irq is sampled as an asynchronous machine interrupt source routed through CSR/trap control.
behavior_contract.output_timing: Observable outputs update on clock boundaries according to boot sequencing, fetch progression, memory stalls, branch/trap redirects, and fail-stop entry. During stalls, externally visible request/address outputs hold stable as required by the subordinate units.
behavior_contract.retention_rules: Once fail_stop asserts, all externally visible CPU request outputs are quiesced or held in terminal safe state until reset. During memory and multiply waits, fetch-facing outputs retain the held PC behavior required by the microarchitecture.
behavior_contract.completion_semantics: Boot success transitions the core from SPI boot into normal instruction fetch from the returned boot PC. Illegal instructions, interrupts, and MRET cause only externally visible redirect behavior through subsequent instruction fetch addresses; internal datapath values are not part of the top-level contract.
forbidden_assumptions: Do not assume internal register or ALU results are externally visible at top level.
Do not assume instruction and data memories are single-cycle.
Do not assume boot_mode_spi can be ignored after reset release.
Do not assume fail-stop permits later fetch or data traffic without reset.
Do not require proof of hidden regfile contents, hidden ALU result buses, or hidden CSR state at top level unless exposed by a port.
Treat datapath-class top-level tests as observable integration flows unless the final value is visible at the top interface.

external_interfaces.name: instruction_fetch_if
external_interfaces.description: Observable request/ready instruction fetch interface exposed at top level.

external_interfaces.name: data_memory_if
external_interfaces.description: Observable variable-latency load/store interface exposed at top level.

external_interfaces.name: spi_boot_if
external_interfaces.description: Observable SPI boot read interface exposed at top level.

external_interfaces.name: fail_stop_if
external_interfaces.description: Terminal boot failure indicator for integration-level verification.

checks.condition: after reset is released and fetch becomes active
first observable fetch after reset release
same cycle as first fetch request
same cycle as first fetch request

timing_contract.notes: Sample on the first active cycle after rst_n rises.

timing_contract.notes: Observe the first active boot cycle.

after boot success transitions to fetch mode
first observable fetch after successful SPI boot

timing_contract.notes: Allow one cycle for boot-complete handoff into fetch.

testcases.expected: Boot start issues first SPI header fetch from address 0 with no completion or fail-stop yet.

timing_contract.notes: Observe first boot request cycle after start.

testcases.stimulus: clk toggling; rst_n=1; start_i=1; spi_ready_i=1; spi_rdata_i=0xB007CAFE; iram_ready_i=0; validation_enable_i=1
testcases.expected: Valid boot header is accepted, SPI fetch continues, and fail-stop remains low.

checks.condition: after valid header word is accepted and boot continues

timing_contract.notes: Header acceptance advances the FSM to the next boot fetch.

testcases.stimulus: clk toggling; rst_n=1; start_i=1; spi_ready_i=1; spi_rdata_i=0x11223344; iram_ready_i=1; validation_enable_i=0
testcases.expected: Program data word is written into IRAM with matching write data and no fail-stop.

checks.condition: when a program word is being copied and IRAM accepts it
when iram_we_o is asserted for this copy word

timing_contract.notes: IRAM write is sampled on the accepted copy cycle.

testcases.stimulus: clk toggling; rst_n=1; start_i=1; spi_ready_i=1; spi_rdata_i=0x00000020; iram_ready_i=1; validation_enable_i=0
testcases.expected: Boot completes successfully with boot PC 0x20 and no fail-stop.

timing_contract.notes: done_o may pulse or remain asserted in a completed state; the sampled success cycle must match.

testcases.stimulus: clk toggling; rst_n=1; start_i=1; spi_ready_i=1; spi_rdata_i=0xFFFFFFFF; iram_ready_i=0; validation_enable_i=1
testcases.expected: Malformed header causes terminal fail-stop, suppresses done, and stops SPI requests.

checks.condition: in fail-stop state after malformed header

timing_contract.notes: fail_stop_o is terminal until reset.

testcases.stimulus: clk toggling; rst_n=1; start_i=1; spi_ready_i=1; spi_rdata_i=0x00010000; iram_ready_i=0; validation_enable_i=1
testcases.expected: Out-of-range program size causes terminal fail-stop and no done.

timing_contract.notes: fail_stop_o is terminal until reset.

testcases.stimulus: clk toggling; rst_n=1; start_i=1; spi_ready_i=0; spi_rdata_i=0x00000000; iram_ready_i=0; validation_enable_i=1
testcases.expected: If SPI response timeout/error policy expires, terminal fail-stop asserts and done remains low.

checks.condition: after the implementation-defined timeout/error window expires

timing_contract.notes: TB must wait the design's timeout interval or a bounded watchdog and then sample fail-stop.
